sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Randy Gutierrez, a 2-year-old male, presented with symptoms of a runny nose, stuffiness, and a cough that is not barky, starting around supper time last night.
His mother reported that he has been pulling on his ears and rubbing his nose.
The patient has a low-grade fever of 100.3°F.
He ate his supper and breakfast, but only consumed a small amount of food.
He has been irritable and has difficulty sleeping.
His mother also reported that his older sister has been sick, and they had a playdate with a nephew who had a sinus infection.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient has a runny nose, stuffy nose, cough, pulling on his ears, and rubbing his nose.
He has a low-grade fever.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking Tylenol for fever and has used saline mist, baby Vicks, and a humidifier.
The mother has been using Aveno shampoo on his scalp.
Objective:
Vital signs: Temperature 100.3°F (rectal), pulse rate not mentioned, blood pressure not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, and respiratory rate not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's ears appear okay, and his mucous is clear.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a viral upper respiratory infection.
Problem list: Upper respiratory infection; suspected viral upper respiratory infection.
Plan:
I prescribed the same medication as his sister, which is a half a teaspoon of medication for a barky cough, to be given if the patient develops a barky cough.
I also recommended Zarbee's for his stuffy nose.
The mother should continue to use saline mist, baby Vicks, and a humidifier.
For his cradle cap, I recommended using a shampoo such as Head & Shoulders, Selsun Blue, or Nizoral AD, and to use a soft brush and baby oil to remove the scales.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again in one week.
